---
title: ""
page-layout: full
---

::: page-header
# Build your own academic website
:::

::: profile-links-row
[View the template on GitHub](https://github.com/ccicm/academic-website-template){.profile-link}
:::

This is the template and setup I used to build this site. It's free, requires no coding experience, and takes about 30–60 minutes from start to a live website. Everything below walks you through each step.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## What you'll end up with

A professional academic website with pages for Research & Publications, Education & Experience, Projects, and Contact — automatically deployed every time you push a change. Publications sync live from your ORCID profile with journal favicons, APA 7 citations, and RIS downloads built in. A reading list pulled from a simple JSON file appears on both the home and research pages. The contact page uses a Web3Forms form — no backend required. Hosted for free on GitHub Pages, with the option to add your own custom domain (e.g. `yourname.com`).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## What you'll need

::: role-section
::: role-block
[A GitHub account]{.role-title}
[Free at [github.com](https://github.com). This is where your site lives.]{.role-desc}
:::

::: role-block
[GitHub Desktop]{.role-title}
[Free at [desktop.github.com](https://desktop.github.com). The easiest way to manage your site files without using the command line.]{.role-desc}
:::

::: role-block
[Quarto]{.role-title}
[Free at [quarto.org](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/). The tool that turns your text files into a website.]{.role-desc}
:::

::: role-block
[RStudio (optional but recommended)]{.role-title}
[Free at [posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/). The easiest editor for Quarto files. You don't need to know R — it's just the best interface for this kind of site.]{.role-desc}
:::
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Step 1 — Get the template

1. Go to the [template repository](https://github.com/ccicm/academic-website-template) on GitHub
2. Click the green **Use this template** button → **Create a new repository**
3. Name your repository (e.g. `yourname-website` or just `yourname`)
4. Make sure it's set to **Public** — this is required for free GitHub Pages hosting
5. Click **Create repository**

You now have your own copy of the template in your GitHub account.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Step 2 — Enable GitHub Pages

1. In your new repository, click **Settings** (top right of the repo nav)
2. In the left sidebar, click **Pages**
3. Under **Build and deployment**, set Source to **GitHub Actions**
4. That's it — the workflow file already in the template handles the rest

Your site will build automatically. After 1–2 minutes, a URL will appear at the top of the Pages settings screen: `https://yourgithubusername.github.io/your-repo-name`.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Step 3 — Download the files to your computer

1. Open **GitHub Desktop**
2. Click **File → Clone Repository**
3. Find your new repository in the list and clone it to a folder on your computer
4. Open the folder in RStudio: **File → Open Project** → select the `.Rproj` file, or just open RStudio and navigate to the folder

You'll see all the site files in the file browser on the right side of RStudio.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Step 4 — Edit your content

All the content lives in `.qmd` files — plain text files with a little formatting syntax. Just replace the placeholder text with your own.

::: role-section
::: role-block
[`index.qmd` — Home page]{.role-title}
[Replace `Your Name`, the tag pills, and the bio paragraph. Update the photo path once you've added your photo to the `images/` folder. Edit the stats strip to reflect your own numbers and milestones.]{.role-desc}
:::

::: role-block
[`pages/research.qmd` — Research & Publications]{.role-title}
[Update the research focus paragraph, the PhD status strip, and the paper cards. Then find the line `const ORCID_ID = '...'` near the bottom of the page and replace it with your own ORCID identifier — publications will load and update automatically from there. Also update the `SCHOLAR_URL` constant to your Google Scholar profile URL. Update the sidebar links with your own profile URLs.]{.role-desc}
:::

::: role-block
[`pages/history.qmd` — Education & Experience]{.role-title}
[Fill in your education history, academic roles, clinical or professional roles, memberships, and skills. Each role block follows the same pattern — copy and paste to add more.]{.role-desc}
:::

::: role-block
[`pages/projects.qmd` — Projects]{.role-title}
[Optional page for side projects, tools, or works in progress. Each card has a status badge (Live / In progress / Planned), tag pills, and link buttons. Remove the page from `_quarto.yml` if you don't need it.]{.role-desc}
:::

::: role-block
[`pages/contact.qmd` — Contact form]{.role-title}
[The contact form uses Web3Forms — get a free access key at [web3forms.com](https://web3forms.com) and replace `YOUR-ACCESS-KEY` in the hidden input field. Submissions will be sent to the email address you register with Web3Forms.]{.role-desc}
:::

::: role-block
[`_quarto.yml` — Site title and navigation]{.role-title}
[Change the `title` field to your name. Update the LinkedIn URL in the `right:` section of the navbar. Add or remove nav items to match your pages.]{.role-desc}
:::
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Step 5 — Connect your ORCID

Publications on the Research page load live from ORCID — no manual list to maintain. To connect your own profile:

1. Make sure your ORCID profile is set to **public** and your works are added at [orcid.org](https://orcid.org)
2. Open `pages/research.qmd` and find these two lines near the bottom of the page:

```js
const ORCID_ID    = 'YOUR-ORCID-ID';
const SCHOLAR_URL = 'https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=YOURID';
```

3. Replace `YOUR-ORCID-ID` with your own ORCID (the 16-digit number from your ORCID profile URL, e.g. `0000-0000-0000-0000`)
4. Replace the Scholar URL with your own Google Scholar profile URL

The page will now show your 6 most recent publications, each with a journal favicon, linked journal name, Copy APA 7 button, and Download RIS button. A "View all on Google Scholar" link appears at the bottom.

**Note:** If your ORCID profile is empty or private, the publications block will show a fallback message. You can verify your ORCID is public by visiting `https://pub.orcid.org/v3.0/YOUR-ORCID-ID/works` in a browser — if it returns data, the page will load correctly.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Step 6 — Set up the contact form

The contact page uses [Web3Forms](https://web3forms.com) to receive messages without any backend or server.

1. Go to [web3forms.com](https://web3forms.com) and enter your email address to get a free access key
2. Open `pages/contact.qmd` and find this line:

```html
<input type="hidden" name="access_key" value="YOUR-ACCESS-KEY">
```

3. Replace `YOUR-ACCESS-KEY` with the key from Web3Forms

Submissions will be delivered to the email address you registered with Web3Forms.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Step 7 — Add your reading list

The homepage and research page show a curated list of papers you've been reading, pulled from `data/reading.json`. To update it:

1. Open `data/reading.json`
2. Add entries in this format:

```json
[
  {
    "doi": "10.1000/xyz123",
    "note": "A short note about why this paper is worth reading."
  }
]
```

The homepage shows the first 3 entries; the research page shows up to 5, with a "show more" button for the rest. Metadata (title, authors, journal, year) is fetched automatically from Crossref using the DOI.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Step 8 — Add your photo

1. Find a headshot photo (square or portrait crops work best)
2. Rename it `profile.jpg` (or any name you like)
3. Put it in the `images/` folder inside your project
4. In `index.qmd`, update the image line to match your filename:

```
![Your Name](images/profile.jpg){.profile-img}
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Step 9 — Preview locally

Before publishing, you can preview your site on your own computer.

In RStudio: open the Terminal (**Tools → Terminal → New Terminal**) and run:

```
quarto preview
```

A browser window will open showing your site. It updates live as you edit and save files. Press **Ctrl+C** in the Terminal to stop the preview.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Step 10 — Publish your changes

1. Open **GitHub Desktop**
2. You'll see all the files you've changed listed on the left
3. At the bottom left, type a short summary (e.g. `update bio`) and click **Commit to main**
4. Click **Push origin** at the top

Your site will rebuild automatically and be live within about 2 minutes. Check the **Actions** tab in your GitHub repository to watch it deploy.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Optional: Change the colours

Open `styles.css` and find these two variables near the top:

```css
--navy:   #1a2e4a;   /* headings, buttons, navbar */
--accent: #c8922a;   /* highlights, labels, hover states */
```

Replace the hex colour codes with any colours you like. A few examples:

| Palette | `--navy` | `--accent` |
|---|---|---|
| Navy & amber (default) | `#1a2e4a` | `#c8922a` |
| Deep green & terracotta | `#1e3a2a` | `#c05a4a` |
| Deep green & gold | `#1a3a2a` | `#b8860b` |
| Slate & teal | `#1a2a3a` | `#2a8a7a` |
| Plum & coral | `#3a1a3a` | `#c8522a` |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Optional: Custom domain

If you want your site at `yourname.com` instead of the default GitHub URL:

1. Buy a domain from [Namecheap](https://www.namecheap.com), [Cloudflare](https://www.cloudflare.com), or similar (~$15–25 AUD/year for a `.com`)
2. In your domain registrar's DNS settings, add these four A records and one CNAME:

| Type | Host | Value |
|---|---|---|
| A Record | @ | 185.199.108.153 |
| A Record | @ | 185.199.109.153 |
| A Record | @ | 185.199.110.153 |
| A Record | @ | 185.199.111.153 |
| CNAME Record | www | yourgithubusername.github.io |

3. Create a file called `CNAME` (no extension) in the root of your repo containing exactly your domain name:

```
yourname.com
```

4. In GitHub: **Settings → Pages → Custom domain** → enter `yourname.com` → Save → tick **Enforce HTTPS** once it appears

DNS propagation takes up to 30–60 minutes.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Troubleshooting

::: role-section
::: role-block
[Site not showing after enabling Pages]{.role-title}
[Check the **Actions** tab in your repo. If there's a red X, click it to see the error. The most common cause is an unclosed `:::` block in a `.qmd` file — every `:::` that opens a div needs a matching `:::` to close it.]{.role-desc}
:::

::: role-block
[Changes not appearing on the live site]{.role-title}
[Make sure you committed **and** pushed in GitHub Desktop. A commit alone doesn't send changes to GitHub — you need to click **Push origin** too.]{.role-desc}
:::

::: role-block
[Publications not loading]{.role-title}
[Check that your ORCID profile is set to public and that your works are visible without logging in. Visit `https://pub.orcid.org/v3.0/YOUR-ORCID-ID/works` in a browser — if it returns data, the page will load correctly.]{.role-desc}
:::

::: role-block
[Contact form not delivering messages]{.role-title}
[Double-check the access key in `pages/contact.qmd` matches the one from your Web3Forms account. Also check your spam folder for the first few messages — Web3Forms emails sometimes land there initially.]{.role-desc}
:::

::: role-block
[Custom domain showing as insecure]{.role-title}
[Wait 30–60 minutes after setting up DNS for GitHub to provision the SSL certificate. The **Enforce HTTPS** checkbox will become available once it's ready.]{.role-desc}
:::

::: role-block
[Photo not showing]{.role-title}
[Check the filename and path in `index.qmd` match exactly, including capitalisation. File paths are case-sensitive on GitHub even if they aren't on your computer.]{.role-desc}
:::
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: about-section
Questions or issues with the template? Feel free to [get in touch](pages/contact.qmd) or [open an issue on GitHub](https://github.com/ccicm/academic-website-template/issues/new).
:::
